user_prompt: work on `lib\AudioFifo\AudioFifo.h`, add a publicly accessible way to enable or disable queuing. Dequeuing will still work regardless. The caller of queue does not have to care about checking the flag, the queued data will simply be discarded
also add a getFillPercentagle that returns a number 0 to 100.
agent_reply: When queuing is disabled, `queue()` now discards incoming data and returns `sampleCount`, so producers don’t need to check the flag. Dequeuing is unchanged and continues to drain existing buffered audio.
I also ran `pio run`; the build got past this header change but failed because `EspFileManager` now requires missing `AsyncTCP.h`.

user_prompt: edit `bool grow_file(File &file, uint64_t size)` and limit it to 2GB max
agent_reply: Updated [mic_sd_recorder_demo.cpp](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/src/mic_sd_recorder_demo.cpp:21) so `grow_file(File &file, uint64_t size)` clamps requested growth to `2 GiB` before attempting reservation.
I did a narrow diff check; no other recorder behavior was changed.

user_prompt: fix compilation issues in main.cpp if there is any
agent_reply: Fixed the compilation issue in [src/main.cpp](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/src/main.cpp:5): the forward declaration for `loopTask_core0` was missing its semicolon.

I ran `pio run`; `src/main.cpp` now compiles, and the build proceeds past it. The remaining build failure is unrelated: `EspFileManager` includes missing `AsyncTCP.h`, so PlatformIO needs an AsyncTCP dependency added before the full project can link cleanly.

agent_reply: - Added `AudioFifo::dequeueMonoImmediate(...)` so recorder stop can drain FIFO contents even when normal watermark gating would hold data
Verification: `pio run` compiled `AudioFileRecorder.cpp` and the updated demo. The full build still fails at the existing unrelated `EspFileManager` dependency issue: missing `AsyncTCP.h`.

agent_reply: Added the async web stack dependencies to [platformio.ini](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/platformio.ini):
